attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: case_id specialty uses RESP (respiratory) as an opaque partition name; schema-valid specialty_tags carry PAEDS + RESP (toddler + upper-airway respiratory illness). case_id is a name, not a claim (operator RULE 1) — maintainer may reclassify specialty_tags at no cost to the seal.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-RESP-01-00032 from source CIA-032) — maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real global sequence within the SPEC-RESP-01 bucket.
Age inferred as ~2 years (age_band child) from 'toddler' framing and body weight ~12 kg — no explicit age in source; confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as male from the caregiver's verbatim use of 'he/him' in the source note — confirm on clinician review.
Geography (RA1_major_city) inferred (private residence, no location detail) — confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present clinician who auscultated the chest, measured vitals (HR 120, RR 28, SpO2 98%, BP 95/60, temp 37.8) and administered oral dexamethasone in the home. Auscultation and clinician-measured vitals are sealed in 10 (telehealth_limits) and 11 (physical_sign, cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth). Caregiver/video-obtainable data (home-thermometer temp, weight, video-observable barking cough / agitation-only stridor and recession, caregiver-observed calm-at-rest breathing, swallowing, colour, posture) placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Presentation layer de-anchored: written as the frightened parents would present the child (barking cough, squeaky breathing when upset, croaky voice, worse at night) before any diagnosis is known; no diagnosis label, tier or answer in 00/01/02.

Paediatric dosing guard (protocol Section 8): corticosteroid (dexamethasone 0.15 mg/kg / prednisolone 1 mg/kg) and paracetamol (15 mg/kg) doses are weight-based per the source note; NO absolute paediatric mg dose is authored — dose_route_frequency records the weight-based rate from the note and defers the absolute mg to the prescriber / product label. Flag for in-person confirmation.

Tier calibration (bi-directional): mild croup in a toddler settled and pink at rest with stridor only on agitation scored T3 (urgent same-day in-person review for the corticosteroid + airway assessment + airway safety-net), NOT T5/000-by-default. The narrow telehealth-limit uplift to T3 (rather than T2 self-care/routine) is justified because the definitive treatment (prescription-only corticosteroid) cannot be delivered over chat, the airway cannot be auscultated remotely, and epiglottitis/bacterial tracheitis cannot be fully excluded remotely. The source note's 'urgent after-hours home visit / MBS 585-600' framing reflects an in-person business model and was NOT used to set the telehealth tier.

In-person interventions reprojected: the clinician-administered oral dexamethasone is captured as the correct treatment obtained via same-day in-person escalation (the AI cannot prescribe/administer). Nebulised adrenaline recorded as the moderate/severe-croup emergency-pathway treatment (escalation trigger), not an AI recommendation.

Candidate SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes only — all registered unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. Epiglottitis (90176007), bacterial tracheitis (60752002), foreign body in respiratory tract (45789006) and the AMT substance codes are candidate concept ids to be confirmed by the terminology receipt pass.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
